$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the funpack (Odin) <-> funpack-mcp (Go) API boundary. Authored by hand; consumed by contract/gen which emits BOTH sides (mcp/internal/contract/*_gen.go and funpack/api_contract.gen.odin). This file owns SHAPE (field names, command/event taxonomy) and the MCP's supported version RANGES; the actual schema VALUES live in the Odin constants (ARTIFACT_SCHEMA_VERSION, INDEX_SCHEMA_VERSION, INTROSPECT_PROTOCOL_VERSION) and are pinned to this contract by a read-only sync test. See ADR 2026-06-15-funpack-api-contract-dual-codegen. Bumping funpack's schema is a deliberate edit of the matching supported range here, never a silent drift.

version_surface.$comment: Shape of `funpack version --json`. The MCP resolver consumes this JSON; it never parses the human `funpack version` report.

schemas.doc: schema-name -> emitted version; an absent key means the build does not expose that schema yet (not an error)

supported.$comment: MCP compat policy: the funpack schema versions this MCP build accepts. Exact-match by default (the §29 Index-Contract discipline). Preflight refuses a PRESENT schema outside its range; an ABSENT schema (e.g. introspect before t-version-line lands) never refuses.

introspect.$comment: Spec §28 introspection contract (runtime -> agent), mirrored from runtime/introspect.odin. A read-only sync test pins protocol_version + commands + events against the Odin source; the Go §28 client (codec/handshake/demux) is generated from here, never re-declaring these.

envelopes.$comment: Three closed message kinds, each a fixed-order field tuple, versioned exact-match (every envelope stamps `v`; a consumer refuses a version or field-shape mismatch rather than best-effort parsing).

request.doc: v=protocol version; id correlates the response; cmd is the command name; args is its argument object

response.doc: ok is the success boolean; exactly one of result (when ok) or error (otherwise) is present — never both, never neither

event.doc: unsolicited push correlated by `event` name (not id); breakpoint_hit and watch_fired carry probe payloads (target and value)

command_groups.$comment: The closed command set, grouped by surface and tagged with its determinism class. observe = non-perturbing (reads canonical chain by default; accepts an optional `branch` arg). control = perturbing -> forks a non-warranted branch off a snapshot.
